name: scenario_042d6d_environment_recall_notice_arrives_cedar_lib_eq
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q4 2025: 10 critical issues have surfaced over 24 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Environment. The immediate incident is a recall notice landing mid-operations. The trigger is that a recall notice arrived mid-operations, and the team must decide what to pause without collapsing service delivery. The board has pre-approved up to $10.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as liberty vs equality: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a same-day board briefing. Boundaries: No one is denied service solely due to an automated flag; a human fallback must exist. If the vendor cannot provide logs within 2 hours, the plan must assume worst-case exposure. All exceptions require a written ticket with a reason code. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Incident Director Keisha Diallo
agents.identity: I am Incident Director Keisha Diallo, coordinating the Environment response at Cedar Environment Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want a decision that is bounded, reversible, and measurable—so we can move quickly without losing the ability to stop.

agents.name: Patient Safety Liaison Talia Patel
agents.identity: I am Patient Safety Liaison Talia Patel, coordinating the Environment response at Cedar Environment Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with two-person sign-off for overrides, weekly subgroup error-rate disclosure, and independent log capture; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Incident Director Keisha Diallo must choose an authorization approach for this Environment decision. What does Incident Director Keisha Diallo do?
